content.problemStatement:
You are given TTT testcases , in each testcase you are given three numbers AAA, BBB and CCC .
 Find that whether an expression of the form ‘‘xAyBzC"`` x \hspace{0.2em} A \hspace{0.2em} y \hspace{0.2em} B \hspace{0.2em} z \hspace{0.2em} C "‘‘xAyBzC" exists ( where xxx , yyy and zzz can be +++ or −-− ) , such that the final result is 000. If it exists print ‘‘YES"``YES"‘‘YES" (without quotes), else print ‘‘NO"``NO"‘‘NO"(without quotes).


Input Format

First line contains TTT , Number of Testcases 

Now each test case contains 3 integers AAA,BBB,CCC


Output Format

For each testcase print one line consisting of ‘‘YES"``YES"‘‘YES" or ‘‘NO"``NO"‘‘NO" (without quotes) as described in problem.

Output is Case-Insensitive i.e. ‘‘Yes"``Yes"‘‘Yes" , ‘‘nO"``nO"‘‘nO" , ‘‘YeS"``YeS"‘‘YeS" all will be considered correct.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to assign a ‘+’ or ‘‑’ sign before each of A, B and C so that the resulting expression equals 0, otherwise output "NO". The output is case‑insensitive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: No pair of numbers sums to the third in either test case, so the answer is NO for both.

solution.approach: Because all numbers are positive, the only way to obtain a sum of zero with signs ± is when one number equals the sum of the other two. Therefore the answer is YES iff A + B == C or A + C == B or B + C == A. We check these three conditions for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
